description: You are a newly hired backend developer at a fintech company. Harden your Firefox browser configuration according to the company's security baseline by applying the following security preferences: `network.dns.disablePrefetch` (true), `network.prefetch-next` (false), `media.peerconnection.enabled` (false), `geo.enabled` (false), `privacy.resistFingerprinting` (true), `network.http.sendRefererHeader` (0), `browser.send_pings` (false), and `webgl.disabled` (true). Additionally, set Enhanced Tracking Protection to Strict mode and the homepage to https://developer.mozilla.org. Document these changes in a JSON configuration audit report at ~/Documents/browser_security_config.json including the audit date (YYYY-MM-DD), the policy name "Fintech Browser Hardening Baseline", an object named `about_config_changes` containing the eight preference names and their applied values, the tracking protection status ("strict"), and the homepage URL.
